title: Gravitational acceleration
short: The acceleration of free fall, g ≈ 9.81 m/s² near Earth's surface.
full:
Near Earth's surface every freely falling object accelerates downward at the same rate, g ≈ 9.81 m/s², regardless of its mass (ignoring air resistance). This is the gravitational field strength: it also equals the gravitational force per unit mass, so the weight of a mass m is W = m·g.

The value varies slightly with location and altitude and is different on other bodies (about 1.6 m/s² on the Moon).

The SI unit is m/s² (or equivalently N/kg when viewed as field strength).
